'''Daniel Levi''' (born 5 December 1995) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]] He has played for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]], [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] and [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] at international level.

He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]], [[Brisbane Broncos]], [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] and the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Levi was born in [[Wellington]], New Zealand, and is of [[Māori people|Māori]] and [[Samoans|Samoan]] descent.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.tvnz.co.nz/one-news/sport/league/guess-coaches-have-seen-something-in-me-kiwis-hooker-hoping-impress-after-taking-heros-spot|title='I guess the coaches have seen something in me' - Kiwis hooker hoping to impress after taking hero's spot|via=TVNZ|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180203003923/https://www.tvnz.co.nz/one-news/sport/league/guess-coaches-have-seen-something-in-me-kiwis-hooker-hoping-impress-after-taking-heros-spot |archive-date=3 February 2018}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Randwick Kingfishers]]. He then moved to Australia and played for the [[Glenmore Park Brumbies]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.brumbiesstampede.com/about/|title=ABOUT US - Penrith Junior Rugby League Club | Glenmore Park Brumbies JRLFC|publisher=Brumbiesstampede.com|access-date=2015-09-05|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150923194810/http://www.brumbiesstampede.com/about/|archive-date=23 September 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref> before being signed by the [[Newcastle Knights]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Midway through the 2013 season, Levi signed with the Newcastle Knights on a {{frac|2|1|2}}-year contract effective immediately.<ref name="newcastleknights1">{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/09/24/levi_extends_knights_contract.html|title=Levi extends Knights contract - Knights|date=24 September 2014|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-06-21}}</ref> From 2013 to 2015, he played for the Knights' [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team,<ref>{{cite web|url=http://nycdatabase.org/players/l/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150616162758/http://nycdatabase.org/players/l/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=16 June 2015|title=L|date=27 December 2012|publisher=Nycdatabase.org|access-date=2015-06-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/03/24/nyc_and_nsw_cup_roun.html|title=NYC and NSW Cup round 4 teams - Knights|date=24 March 2015|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-06-21}}</ref> captaining the side in 2014 as an 18-year-old and also in 2015.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2014/10/21/kiwi_representation_motivates_levi.html|title=Kiwi experience motivates Levi - Knights|date=21 October 2014|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-06-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/knights-v-panthers-five-key-points/tabid/10874/newsid/84903/default.aspx|title=Knights v Panthers: Five key points|publisher=NRL.com|date=2015-03-28|access-date=2015-06-21}}</ref> On 2 September 2014, he was named on the interchange bench in the 2014 NYC Team of the Year.<ref>{{cite web|author=Steven Russo|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|title=Holden Cup Team Of The Year Announced|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2014-09-02|access-date=2015-05-31|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150626101814/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/holden-team-year-announced/|archive-date=26 June 2015|df=dmy-all}}</ref> On 24 September 2014, he extended his contract with the Knights to the end of 2017.<ref name="newcastleknights1"/> On 18 October 2014, he played for the [[Junior Kiwis]] against the [[Junior Kangaroos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2014-junior-kiwis-team-announced/|title=2014 JUNIOR KIWIS TEAM ANNOUNCED|publisher=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2014-10-10|access-date=2015-03-13|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150402092606/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/2014-junior-kiwis-team-announced/|archive-date=2 April 2015|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

===2015===
Levi played for the Knights in the pre-season [[2015 NRL Auckland Nines|Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/|title=KNIGHTS NAME 2015 NINES SQUAD|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-01-20|access-date=2015-12-21|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160130234832/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-2015-nines-squad/|archive-date=30 January 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

On 2 May, he again played for the Junior Kiwis, coming off the interchange bench in the Kiwis' 22-20 loss to the Junior Kangaroos.<ref name="autogenerated1"/><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/representative-round-team-lists/tabid/10874/newsid/85557/default.aspx|title=Representative Round team lists|website=NRL.com|date=2015-04-28|access-date=2015-12-20}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/junior-roos-hold-on-in-thriller/tabid/10874/newsid/85888/default.aspx|title=Junior Roos hold on in thriller|website=NRL.com|date=2015-05-02|access-date=2015-12-20}}</ref> 
[[File:Dannylevi.jpg|thumb|upright|Levi playing for the Newcastle Knights in 2015]]
In Round 15 of the [[2015 NRL season|season]], he made his first-grade debut for the Knights against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]], playing off the interchange bench in the Knights' 30-28 loss at [[Newcastle International Sports Centre|Hunter Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2015/06/21/late_mail_knights_v_.html|title=LATE MAIL: Knights v Sharks - Knights|date=21 June 2015|publisher=Newcastleknights.com.au|access-date=2015-06-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last=Keeble|first=Brett|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/3169251/levi-realises-his-dream/|title=Levi realises his dream | Newcastle Herald|website=Theherald.com.au|date=2015-06-24|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/cronulla-sharks-beat-newcastle-knights-30-28-as-ben-barba-puts-in-impressive-cameo/story-fni3fbgz-1227408397949|title=Sharks Beat Knights 30-28 as Ben Barba Puts in Impressive Cameo|website=Dailytelegraph.com.au|date=2015-06-26|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> In Round 16 against the [[Brisbane Broncos]], he scored his first NRL career try in the Knights' 44-22 loss at [[Lang Park|Suncorp Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/rugby-league-match-centre/live-nrl-brisbane-broncos-v-newcastle-knights-20150626-ghywgq.html|title=Live NRL: Brisbane Broncos v Newcastle Knights|website=Smh.com.au|date=2015-06-26|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> On 27 August, he extended his contract with the Knights to the end of 2018.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/levi-and-cogger-extend-contracts-18754/#mMlUowbiqL9gwy2m.97|title=Levi and Cogger extend contracts | NRL|publisher=Zero Tackle|date=2015-08-27|access-date=2015-09-04|archive-date=22 December 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151222152422/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/levi-and-cogger-extend-contracts-18754/#mMlUowbiqL9gwy2m.97|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-re-sign-levi-cogger/|title=Knights Re-Sign Levi, Cogger|publisher=Rugby League Week|date=2015-08-27|access-date=2015-12-21|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151222104114/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-re-sign-levi-cogger/|archive-date=22 December 2015|url-status=dead}}</ref>

Levi played at hooker in the Knights' [[2015 New South Wales Cup#Chart|New South Wales Cup Grand Final]] win over the [[Wyong Roos]],<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nswrl.com.au/news/2015/09/22/vb_nsw_cup_grand_fin.html|title=VB NSW Cup Grand Final Team List|date=22 September 2015|publisher=NSWRL|access-date=2015-10-15}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Brett Keeble|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/newcastle-knights/newcastle-knights-extend-clint-newtons-career-with-nsw-cup-win-over-wyong-roos-20150927-gjw2gs.html|title=Newcastle Knights extend Clint Newton's career with NSW Cup win over Wyong Roos|date=27 September 2015|publisher=Smh.com.au|access-date=2015-10-15}}</ref> and the following week in the [[NRL State Championship]] Final, a 12-26 loss against the [[Ipswich Jets]].<ref>{{cite web|author=Brett Keeble|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/ipswich-jets-shoot-down-newcastle-knights-to-win-nrl-state-championship-final-20151004-gk0zw3.html|title=Ipswich Jets shoot down Newcastle Knights to win NRL State Championship Final|website=Smh.com.au|date=2015-10-04|access-date=2015-12-21}}</ref> He finished off his debut year in the NRL having played in 7 matches and scoring 2 tries for the Knights as the club finished last.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY2MC00LS0tLS0tMjE3MTEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|date=2015-11-05|access-date=2015-12-25}}</ref> He was named on the interchange bench in the 2015 [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] Team of the Year for a second year in a row.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/2015-holden-cup-team-of-the-year/tabid/10874/newsid/89792/default.aspx|title=2015 Holden Cup Team of the Year|date=14 September 2015|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=2015-09-18}}</ref>

===2016===
In February, Levi played for the Knights in the [[2016 NRL Auckland Nines]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/|title=Knights Announce Nines Squad - Rugby League Week|website=Rugbyleagueweek.com.au|date=2016-01-28|access-date=2016-02-04|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160204042513/http://rugbyleagueweek.com.au/knights-announce-nines-squad/|archive-date=4 February 2016|df=dmy-all}}</ref> In May, he was named as 19th man for [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]] during the [[2016 Anzac Test]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/bromwich-to-lead-new-look-kiwis/tabid/10874/newsid/95473/default.aspx|title=Bromwich to lead new-look Kiwis|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=2016-09-04|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160914194507/http://www.nrl.com/bromwich-to-lead-new-look-kiwis/tabid/10874/newsid/95473/default.aspx|archive-date=14 September 2016|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.stuff.co.nz/sport/league/79493490/Warriors-hooker-Issac-Luke-dropped-from-Kiwis-Storm-prop-Jesse-Bromwich-named-captain|title=Warriors hooker Issac Luke dropped from Kiwis, Storm prop Jesse Bromwich named captain|publisher=Stuff.co.nz|date=2016-05-01|access-date=2016-09-04}}</ref> He finished the season having played in 18 matches as the club finished last on the table.  Out of 18 matches Levi played in, 17 of them ended in defeat.  The other match was a 24-24 draw with the [[Canberra Raiders]] in round 3 of the competition.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/matches/Custom/MTUtMTY3Ny00LS0tLS0tMjE3MTEtLS0tLS0tLS0tLS0t|title=Custom Match List|publisher=Rugby League Project|access-date=2016-09-04}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|title=The worst teams in NRL history|website=Sporting News|access-date=7 January 2020|archive-date=11 December 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191211141048/https://www.sportingnews.com/au/other-sports/list/the-worst-teams-in-nrl-history-newcastle-knights-rugby-league-wooden-spoon-parramatta-eels-salary-cap-semi-radradra-kieran-foran/1k2c6o18od8431jen234fqd91u/4/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2017===
In April, Levi extended his Newcastle contract from the end of 2018 until the end of 2020.<ref>{{cite web|author=NewcastleKnights.com.au |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/newcastle-knights-re-sign-danny-levi-26175/ |title=Newcastle Knights re-sign Danny Levi |publisher=Zero Tackle |date=2017-03-05 |access-date=2017-04-05}}</ref>

Levi played a total of 24 games for Newcastle in the [[2017 NRL season]] as the club finished last for a third straight year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2017/12/12/knights-2017-by-the-numbers/|title=Knights: 2017 by the numbers|website=NRL|date=12 December 2017}}</ref>

===2018===
[[File:Danny Levi.jpg|thumb|right|Levi playing for the Newcastle Knights in 2018]]
Levi played 17 games for Newcastle in the [[2018 NRL season]] as the club improved upon the earlier three years by finishing 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/05/newcastle-knights-2018-season-review/|title=Newcastle Knights 2018 season review|website=NRL|date=22 October 2018}}</ref>

===2019===
Levi played 17 games in the [[2019 NRL season]] for Newcastle as the club finished 11th on the table for a second consecutive year.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://nothingbutleague.com/2019/10/03/2019-season-review-newcastle-knights/|title=2019 SEASON REVIEW NEWCASTLE KNIGHT|website=Nothing But League|date=3 October 2019|access-date=7 January 2020|archive-date=3 February 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200203133211/https://nothingbutleague.com/2019/10/03/2019-season-review-newcastle-knights/|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===2020===
On 22 January, Levi was released by Newcastle and signed a one-year contract to join the [[Manly Warringah Sea Eagles]] for the [[2020 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/01/22/levis-in-fashion-this-season-manly-like-style-of-new-arrival/|title=Levi's in fashion this season: Manly like style of new arrival|website=NRL|date=22 January 2020}}</ref>

On 26 February, Levi spoke to the media about his departure from Newcastle and his rocky relationship with the club's former head coaching Nathan Brown saying “It was hard, it was a bit of a rollercoaster. I thought there were things that were unfair, You hear a lot of stories where coaches that just don’t like you, no matter what you do. You could be in his bad books and that’s it. I think I might have been in a situation like that.  I'm just happy to be out of it. I never have anything towards the club or the team, some of my best friends are in that team".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/knights/exiled-danny-levi-opens-up-on-unfair-knights-exit-and-rocky-relationship-with-former-coach/news-story/8fdc0ab69630da09a7a114da5f85a89e/|title=Danny Levi opens up on unfair exit|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=26 February 2020}}</ref>

In round 10 against Parramatta, Levi scored his first try for Manly-Warringah as they won the match 22-18 at [[Brookvale Oval]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2020-07-18/sea-eagles-stun-eels-dogs-blow-it-late-knights-hang-on/12469972/|title=Sea Eagles Stun Eels Dogs Blow It Late|newspaper=ABC News|date=18 July 2020}}</ref>

In September, Levi was informed by Manly-Warringah that his services would not be required for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2020-transfer-centre-signings-tracker-adam-blair-retirement-offcontract-players/news-story/841b83e0d982f7588233ca2c3e22701b|title= Bunnies lock in two rising stars; Manly farewell three players|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date= 28 September 2020}}</ref>

===2021===
On 4 February 2021 it was reported that Levi would join the [[Norths Devils]] in the [[Queensland Cup]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=4 Feb 2021|accessdate=5 Feb 2021|title=Danny Levi finds new club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-levi-finds-new-club/}}</ref>

On 20 April, Levi signed with the [[Brisbane Broncos]] for the remainder of the season, later that week, Levi made his club debut in round 7 against [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] where Brisbane lost 46-6 at [[TIO Stadium]] in Darwin.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2021-04-23/nrl-parramatta-brisbane-broncos-south-sydney-gold-coast/100087882/|title=Parramatta thrashes Brisbane Broncos 46-6, Benji Marshall inspires South Sydney to 40-30 NRL win over Gold Coast|newspaper=ABC News|date=23 April 2021}}</ref>
On 29 September, Levi signed a two-year deal to join English side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/12421027/huddersfield-sign-former-new-zealand-hooker-danny-levi-from-brisbane-broncos-on-two-year-deal#/|title=Huddersfield sign former New Zealand hooker Danny Levi from Brisbane Broncos on two-year deal|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===2022===
On 28 May, Levi played for Huddersfield in their [[2022 Challenge Cup Final]] loss to [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/61594770.amp/|title=Challenge Cup final: Huddersfield Giants 14-16 Wigan Warriors|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

In October Levi was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Samoa|Samoa squad]] for the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup – Men's tournament|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021]</ref>

===2023===
On 8 January, Levi signed a two-year deal to join [[Canberra Raiders|Canberra]] ahead of the [[2023 NRL season]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2023/01/08/danny-levi-to-join-raiders/|title=Danny Levi to join Raiders|website=www.raiders.com.au}}</ref>
In round 3 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Levi suffered a broken jaw during Canberra's 24-20 victory over Cronulla and was ruled out for an indefinite period. It was reported that trainers attempted to search for Levi's missing teeth during and after the game.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/danny-levi-forced-search-teeth-26513428/|title=Danny Levi forced to search for his teeth after ex-Huddersfield hooker suffers horror injury|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>
Levi was limited to only four matches with Canberra in the [[2023 NRL season]] as the club finished 8th on the table and qualified for the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-canberra-raiders-season-review-jack-wighton-ricky-stuart-jarrod-croker-finals-knights-game-jamal-fogarty/news-story/4befaf00c9ae2de911aed34c6d80c77f/|title=Ricky turned losing a club legend into a winning season. Now for his biggest challenge - Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2024===
Levi played 23 matches for Canberra in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 9th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-season-review-canberra-raiders-analysis-exclusive-20240911-p5k9ws.html/|title=The Mole's end-of-season review: The 'wasted' enforcer Ricky Stuart must 'fire up' in 2025|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

=== 2025 ===
Levi made only two appearances for Canberra in the [[2025 NRL season]]. On 30 September, Levi was one of six players that departed the capital club at the end of their season.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-09-25 |title=Forever Green: Raiders departing players |url=https://www.raiders.com.au/news/2025/09/25/forever-green-raiders-departing-players/ |access-date=2025-09-25 |website=Canberra Raiders |language=en}}</ref>

On 24 November 2025 it was reported that he signed for the [[Leeds Rhinos]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=24 Nov 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Danny Levi’s first words as Leeds Rhinos switch confirmed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/danny-levis-first-words-as-leeds-rhinos-switch-confirmed}}</ref> On 24 November 2025, Leeds officially confirmed the signing of Levi on a two year deal.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-11-24 |title=Leeds Rhinos sign Samoa hooker Danny Levi from Canberra Raiders |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/czdg1z71q7ro |access-date=2025-11-24 |website=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref>

== Statistics ==
{| class="wikitable"
